notes.note: Appius Claudius Pulcher wrote a learned treatise on augural discipline (the Roman science of reading the gods' will from the flight of birds and other signs), which he dedicated to Cicero, his fellow augur.
One of Appius's daughters married Pompey's elder son, Gnaeus Pompeius the younger; the marriage tie made the great general Appius's connection by marriage.
The future tyrannicide Marcus Brutus had married another of Appius's daughters, Claudia, binding him to Appius as son-in-law.

notes.anchor: our common membership of the college
notes.note: Cicero and Appius were both members of the college of augurs, the priestly board charged with taking the auspices; Appius had supported Cicero's co-option into it in 53 BC.
